title: Phase 3.0 redesign DeltaSetup public project experience
description: Redesign the DeltaSetup public GitHub Pages experience so it is mindblowingly helpful, clear, and visually coherent. Current issues include awkward stat/ticker sections, unclear section purpose, weak rendered contrast in some components, too much decorative narrative without enough decision/action clarity, and inconsistent visual rhythm. Start with experience-architect strategy, then implement focused improvements.
acceptance_criteria: Experience architect review exists; redesign plan is documented; high-impact page sections are refactored for clarity and visual quality; QA/static/browser smoke audits pass; changes pushed to gh-pages.
notes:
Owner/agent: code-puppy-13b0ad. Tyler explicitly requested Phase 3.0 and experience-architect involvement.
web-puppy-0a95fb completed Phase 3.0 B2B/static landing-page research and saved deliverables under research/b2b-static-landing-pages-phase3-redesign/. Findings cover IA, vanity-stat replacement, action-oriented sections, WCAG 2.2 card/button/focus/contrast considerations, and GitHub Pages/no-build constraints.
close_reason: Completed Phase 3.0 pass 1. Invoked experience-architect, documented plan, replaced ugly stats/ticker with Current Decision Brief, simplified hero copy/routing, added Decision Queue, fixed reveal visibility for new decision surfaces, and verified static/browser audits pass. Follow-up passes filed for White Glove/problem evidence compression and MSP homepage routing.

title: Fix rendered MSP page contrast and sidebar view button design
description: The deployed MSP page still has visibly poor rendered contrast and awkward view-switcher buttons despite internal readiness checks. Fix actual visual contrast for Project/Ops/MSP Brief sidebar buttons, MSP hero action buttons, and muted text/card treatments. Validate with browser smoke/static audits and push.
acceptance_criteria: MSP page view switcher is compact and readable; hero action buttons have strong contrast; visible muted copy/cards are readable in rendered screenshots; static and browser smoke audits pass; changes pushed to gh-pages.
notes: Owner/agent: code-puppy-13b0ad. Reported by Tyler with screenshot from https://delta-crown-org.github.io/DeltaSetup/msp.html.
close_reason: Fixed rendered MSP page contrast and navigation button design. Reworked sidebar view switcher into readable stacked pills, increased sidebar text contrast, made MSP hero content immediately visible/readable, strengthened MSP card/body text contrast, bumped cache-busters, and verified static + browser smoke audits pass.

title: Phase 3.0 pass 2: compress White Glove and problem evidence sections
description: Continue the Phase 3.0 homepage redesign by converting oversized narrative/evidence sections into decision-useful digest components. Compress White Glove on index.html into a compact target day-one experience card that links to operations.html, and convert the problem/folder section into an evidence digest with optional details for audited examples.
acceptance_criteria: Homepage White Glove section is compact and action-oriented; problem section presents evidence with implication/action; large decorative evidence is collapsed or moved; audits pass; changes pushed.
notes: Follow-up from experience-architect-session-d987b0. Phase 3.0 pass 1 replaced ticker with Decision Brief and simplified hero routing.
close_reason: Completed Phase 3.0 pass 2: compressed the homepage White Glove scrollytelling into a compact Target Day-One Experience decision card, added explicit current gaps and ops route, replaced the large problem/folder wall with a Current-State Evidence digest, contextual metrics, implication/action cards, and a details disclosure for audited folders. Audits pass.

title: Complete manual WCAG 2.2 AAA certification pass
description: Perform a manual/browser-assisted WCAG 2.2 AAA review of index.html, operations.html, and msp.html before making any public AAA compliance claim. Cover AAA criteria that cannot be fully proven statically: focus not obscured enhanced, link purpose in context/link-only where applicable, section headings, reading level/plain language, animation from interactions, target spacing, keyboard order, screen-reader behavior, and page behavior with browser zoom/text spacing.
acceptance_criteria: Manual checklist completed with evidence, any exceptions documented as not applicable or remediated, and an explicit final claim decision: AAA compliant, AAA-ready with exceptions, or AA only.
notes: Blocked until a human/browser QA pass is scheduled. Static audit and Playwright smoke audit pass as of 2026-05-07.

title: Verify completion status and WCAG 2.2 AAA readiness
description: Audit the current GitHub Pages site after the design-system and MSP brief work. Determine what is complete, what remains, and whether the public pages can honestly claim WCAG 2.2 AAA compliance. Do not claim AAA unless validated against applicable AAA success criteria, including contrast, focus, motion, readability, headings/landmarks, target size, and responsive behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Report created with completion matrix, AAA compliance claim status, verified passes, verified failures/unknowns, and remediation beads for any remaining work.
notes: Owner/agent: code-puppy-13b0ad. Pages in scope: index.html, operations.html, msp.html. Static site deployed from gh-pages.
close_reason: Completed AAA readiness alignment report and remediation pass. Added static and browser smoke audit scripts; both pass. Fixed AAA contrast token gaps, skip-link focus behavior, and heading-order warnings. Formal AAA claim remains pending manual certification in DeltaSetup-9gq; axe/pa11y automation tracked in DeltaSetup-did.

title: Audit and remediate aria-label on non-semantic divs (axe aria-prohibited-attr)
description:
axe-core flags 28 nodes across index.html (19), operations.html (8), msp.html (1) where aria-label is applied to plain <div> elements with no explicit role. Per ARIA in HTML, aria-label is prohibited on generic elements with no role because there is no semantic for the name to apply to. axe marks these incomplete (not violations) because it cannot fully prove implicit name sources, but the underlying rule is real for WCAG 2.2 AAA.

Typical offenders are landmark-ish containers like .hero-systems, .project-route-actions, .decision-grid, .op-command, .op-impact-grid, .msp-status-grid.

For each: choose either (a) add a meaningful role such as role=group, role=region, or role=list with appropriate descendants; (b) replace the <div> with a semantic element (<section aria-labelledby> when a visible heading exists); or (c) drop aria-label if the surrounding context already names the region. Re-run python3 tests/accessibility_axe_audit.py and verify the aria-prohibited-attr incomplete count drops to 0 or is explicitly justified.

Acceptance: axe audit reports 0 aria-prohibited-attr nodes OR remaining nodes have a documented exception in the manual AAA checklist.

title: Phase 3.0 pass 3: replace homepage MSP/RACI explainer with routing card
description: Reduce homepage duplication by replacing the full MSP/CSP explainer/RACI flow with a concise route card pointing to msp.html. Keep homepage focused as a project command brief while preserving MSP responsibility detail on the dedicated MSP page.
acceptance_criteria: Homepage contains concise MSP/CSP routing surface; detailed RACI remains on msp.html; navigation remains clear; audits pass; changes pushed.
close_reason: Completed Phase 3.0 pass 3: replaced the homepage MSP/CSP/RACI explainer with a concise MSP/CSP route card. Homepage now summarizes HTT/Sui Generis/Pax8 seams and routes detailed RACI/acceptance content to msp.html. Audits pass.

title: Add automated axe/pa11y accessibility regression to quality gates
description: Add a browser-based automated accessibility runner (axe-core, pa11y, or equivalent) for the three public pages. The existing static and Playwright smoke scripts are useful but do not cover axe rule checks. This should become part of the regular quality gate before claiming accessibility compliance.
acceptance_criteria: A repeatable command exists, runs against index.html/operations.html/msp.html, fails on violations, and is documented in the repo QA instructions.
notes: Can use Playwright + axe-core or pa11y. Keep dependencies minimal and avoid huge generated artifacts.
